violations.text: An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (sliced bread) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Employee was directed to wash hands and wear gloves, PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Date of last shellfish served was not documented on shellfish tags. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

Cooked rice was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC chose to dispose during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
A bowl of greens was observed stored in cut romaine lettuce in the salad prep unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
Cases of can goods were observed stored on the floor in the storeroom. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
Gasket on prep unit door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer and stand mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Staff cleaned and sanitized during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
